description_long: This is a small, single-story house with a simple rectangular footprint and a dark gable roof. The exterior walls are light-colored, possibly stucco or painted siding, and the house features several windows on each visible side, some with blue shutters. The main entryway is located at the front, marked by a small stoop and a single door, flanked by two conical evergreen trees. The house is surrounded by a number of snow-dusted bushes or small trees, arranged in a loose ring around the property. The roof is steeply pitched, and the overall design is modest and functional, typical of suburban or rural residential architecture. The setting suggests a winter or early spring environment, with the landscaping providing a sense of privacy and enclosure.
description: A small, single-story house with a dark gable roof, light walls, blue-shuttered windows, and a front entry flanked by evergreen trees, surrounded by snow-dusted bushes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, two conical evergreens, and several windows with blue shutters.
description_view_1: Rear view with the back wall, fewer windows, and the surrounding bushes more evenly spaced.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the gable roof, side windows, and the arrangement of bushes along the property edge.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other side of the house, more windows, and the front entry partially visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple front stoop with a single door, flanked by two conical evergreen trees, no porch or overhang
